project: Royal Caribbean DAM — Modal UX & Admin Mode
overview: Four focused UX improvements: (1) an admin mode toggle in the sidebar that gates upload and delete functionality behind a persistent switch; (2) a share button in the detail modal that copies a deep-link URL to the asset; (3) a clean two-mode metadata toggle (Enriched / Original) replacing the current side-by-side layout; (4) a collapsible metadata accordion in the detail modal so the default view is compact.
goals: Gate destructive and upload actions behind an admin mode toggle stored in localStorage
Let users share a direct link to any asset via a copy-to-clipboard share button
Replace the current side-by-side metadata toggle with a clean Enriched / Original pill toggle showing one view at a time
Default the detail modal to a compact view with metadata collapsed behind an accordion
nonGoals: No authentication or real access control — admin mode is a UI convenience toggle only
No server-side sharing or link expiry
No changes to the metadata schema or enrichment pipeline
No changes to the asset grid or filter sidebar filters
successMetrics: Admin mode off (default): Upload button and Delete button are not rendered
Admin mode on: Upload and Delete buttons appear; state survives page refresh
Share button copies a URL containing the asset ID; clicking it shows 'Copied!' for 2 seconds
Opening the shared URL auto-opens the correct asset modal
Metadata toggle shows Enriched values only by default; switching to Original shows only original values — no side-by-side
Metadata section is collapsed by default; clicking the accordion header expands it
uiNotes: Admin toggle: placed at the very bottom of the FilterSidebar, separated by a top border. Label: 'Admin Mode'. Use a CSS toggle switch (checkbox styled as a pill slider). Active state: navy (#001B6B) track, white thumb. Inactive: grey (#D1D5DB) track.
Admin mode state key in localStorage: 'dam_admin_mode', value '1' when on.

Share button: in the detail modal action row (next to Download and Delete). Label: '↗ Share'. Navy outline style (border: 1.5px solid #001B6B, background transparent, color #001B6B). On click: copies window.location.origin + '/?asset=' + asset.id to clipboard. Button swaps to '✓ Copied!' for 2000ms then reverts.

BrowsePage: on mount, read ?asset= from URL query string. If present, set selectedAssetId to that value to auto-open the modal. After modal closes, remove the ?asset= param from the URL (use history.replaceState).

Metadata toggle: replace the current 'Show Original' button with a pill-style two-option toggle. Left option: 'Enriched' (default active). Right option: 'Original'. Active option: navy background (#001B6B) white text. Inactive: white background navy text, navy border. Border-radius 100px on the container, each option is 50% width. Height 28px, font 12px/600.
Enriched mode (default): single Value column. Each row shows enrichedVal if present, else origVal, else grey 'Not enriched'. No gold borders, no Original column.
Original mode: single Value column. Each row shows origVal if present, else grey '—'. No enriched values shown at all.

Metadata accordion: the entire metadata section (toggle + table) is wrapped in a collapsible. The accordion header shows 'Metadata' label on the left and a ▼/▲ chevron on the right. Default state: collapsed. Clicking the header toggles open/closed. The toggle pill and table only render when expanded.

When metadata is collapsed, the right panel shows: title + action buttons, then Tags row displayed as pills (always visible outside the accordion as a quick summary), then the collapsed 'Metadata ▼' accordion header, then CDN URL, then Enrich button.

Tags always-visible summary: parse enriched_tags (or original_tags as fallback) and show as navy pill chips directly below the title row, even when metadata accordion is collapsed. Label 'Tags' in 11px uppercase muted text above the pills.
rules: Admin mode is read from localStorage on app mount. Default is off (key absent or value !== '1').
adminMode state lives in App.jsx and is passed as a prop to Header (controls Upload button visibility) and to AssetDetailModal (controls Delete button visibility).
FilterSidebar receives adminMode and onAdminModeChange props.
The toggle switch in FilterSidebar calls onAdminModeChange(newValue) which updates App state and writes to localStorage.
BrowsePage reads URLSearchParams on mount only. Uses history.replaceState to clean the URL after the modal opens (so sharing the URL again gives a clean link).
Metadata toggle state (enriched/original) resets to 'enriched' each time the modal opens — same as the current show original reset behaviour.
Metadata accordion state (open/closed) resets to closed each time the modal opens.
The Tags always-visible summary uses display_tags (the merged array from the API). If display_tags is empty, the tags row is not rendered.
Original mode shows only origVal — it does NOT fall back to enrichedVal. If origVal is null, show grey '—'.
Enriched mode shows enrichedVal ?? origVal — existing fallback behaviour preserved.

stories.title: Admin mode toggle — localStorage persistence + gate Upload and Delete
stories.description: As an admin user, I want to toggle admin mode from the sidebar so that upload and delete actions are only available when I need them.
acceptanceCriteria: App.jsx holds adminMode state (boolean, default false) initialised from localStorage key 'dam_admin_mode'
App.jsx passes adminMode and onAdminModeChange to BrowsePage which passes them to FilterSidebar and AssetDetailModal
FilterSidebar renders an admin toggle at the bottom, separated by a top border, with label 'Admin Mode' and a pill-style checkbox toggle
Toggle active state: navy (#001B6B) background track, white thumb. Inactive: #D1D5DB track
Clicking the toggle calls onAdminModeChange, updates App state, and writes '1' or removes the key from localStorage
Header Upload button: only rendered when adminMode is true
AssetDetailModal Delete button: only rendered when adminMode is true
Example: page loads with no localStorage key → Upload button absent, Delete button absent
Example: toggle admin mode on → Upload and Delete appear immediately without page refresh
Negative case: refreshing the page with admin mode on keeps admin mode on (localStorage persists)
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes

stories.title: Share button — copy deep-link URL to asset
stories.description: As a content author, I want to copy a direct link to an asset so I can share it with a colleague who can open it immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: AssetDetailModal action row (next to Download button) renders a Share button
Share button style: transparent background, border '1.5px solid #001B6B', color '#001B6B', border-radius 6px, padding '6px 14px', font-size 13px, label '↗ Share'
On click: copies window.location.origin + '/?asset=' + encodeURIComponent(asset.id) to clipboard using navigator.clipboard.writeText
After copy: button label swaps to '✓ Copied!' for 2000ms then reverts to '↗ Share'
BrowsePage reads URLSearchParams('asset') on mount. If present and non-empty, sets selectedAssetId to that value
After setting selectedAssetId from URL param, BrowsePage calls history.replaceState to remove the ?asset= param from the URL
Example: user clicks Share on asset 'allure-aerial.jpg' → URL copied is 'http://localhost:5173/?asset=allure-aerial.jpg'
Example: opening that URL in a new tab auto-opens the detail modal for that asset
Negative case: if the ?asset= param contains an ID that doesn't exist, the modal opens and shows its loading/error state normally
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes

stories.title: Metadata toggle — clean Enriched / Original pill toggle (one view at a time)
stories.description: As a content author, I want to switch between enriched and original metadata views cleanly so I'm not overwhelmed by a two-column comparison table.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the current 'Show Original' button with a two-option pill toggle: left='Enriched', right='Original'
Toggle container: border-radius 100px, border '1.5px solid #001B6B', display inline-flex, height 28px
Each option: 50% width, font 12px/600, cursor pointer, border-radius 100px
Active option: backgroundColor '#001B6B', color '#FFFFFF'
Inactive option: backgroundColor '#FFFFFF', color '#001B6B'
Default active: Enriched
Enriched mode: single 'Value' column. Shows enrichedVal if present, falls back to origVal, falls back to grey italic 'Not enriched'
Original mode: single 'Value' column. Shows origVal only. If null/empty shows grey '—'. Does NOT show enriched values
No side-by-side layout, no Original column, no gold borders in either mode
Toggle state resets to 'enriched' each time the modal opens
Example: opening modal → Enriched tab active, table shows enriched values
Example: clicking Original tab → table shows only original values, enriched values not visible
Negative case: asset with no enriched data → Enriched mode shows original values as fallback; Original mode shows same original values
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes

stories.title: Compact modal — collapsible metadata accordion, always-visible tags, clean key-value layout
stories.description: As a content author, I want the detail modal to open in a compact state with a clean metadata layout so I can see key information immediately without column headers or section labels cluttering the view.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the standalone 'Metadata' section heading text entirely — the accordion header replaces it
Remove the table column headers ('Field', 'Value', 'Original', 'Enriched') — no thead, no header row
Replace the HTML table with a clean div-based key-value list: each row is a flex row with label on left and value on right
Label: 11px, font-weight 600, text-transform uppercase, letter-spacing 0.04em, color #9CA3AF, width 110px, flex-shrink 0
Value: 13px, font-weight 400, color #111827 (or #9CA3AF for empty/not enriched), flex 1
Rows separated by padding only (8px top/bottom per row) — no border lines between rows
The metadata section (toggle + key-value list) is wrapped in a collapsible accordion
Accordion header: full-width row, label 'Details' (13px/600, color #374151) on left, chevron '▼'/'▲' (12px, color #9CA3AF) on right, cursor pointer, padding '8px 0', border-top '1px solid #F3F4F6'
Default state: collapsed (key-value list and toggle not rendered)
Clicking the header toggles open/closed
A Tags summary row is always visible above the accordion header (never inside the accordion)

Tags summary: small 'Tags' label (11px/600 uppercase muted), then display_tags parsed and rendered as navy pill chips (bg rgba(0,32,91,0.07), color #00205B, border-radius 100px, font 11px/500, padding 2px 8px, gap 4px, flex-wrap wrap)
If display_tags is empty or null, the tags summary row is not rendered
Right panel order from top: title+actions row, tags summary (if present), 'Details' accordion header, [expanded: Enriched/Original toggle + key-value list], CDN URL section, Enrich button
Accordion open/closed state resets to closed each time the modal opens
Example: opening modal shows title, action buttons, tag pills, a 'Details ▼' row — clean and uncluttered
Example: clicking 'Details ▼' expands to show the toggle and clean key-value rows with no column headers
Negative case: asset with no tags → tags row not rendered; accordion still works correctly
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes
